title: Getting started with admixr2
text: admixr2 fits pharmacometric PK/PD models summary-level data instead individual records — meta-analysis framework population PK/PD. inputs can digitised aggregate data published studies (means, error bars covariances), previously published PK/PD models, mix . result single unified population model interpretable fixed, random covariate effects, recovered without individual patient data. study supply: E — observed mean vector (one entry per observation time) V — observed covariance matrix (variance vector) n — sample size times — observation time points ev — dosing event table estimators match E V model-predicted counterparts return standard nlmixr2 fit object, established nlmixr2 models apply aggregate statistics publications internal summaries individual records unavailable. Turning published figure E, V n covered vignette("aggregate-data", package = "admixr2"); letting study carry published model input covered vignette("datagen", package = "admixr2"). Four estimators available: adfo natural starting point model screening initial estimates. admc workhorse standard PK models. adgh noise-free alternative admc, efficient number random effects small. adirmc preferred complex ODE systems expensive solves, high-dimensional IIV, poor starting values. See vignette("estimator-comparison", package = "admixr2") detailed comparison.

title: Getting started with admixr2
text: examplomycin ships admixr2: 500 simulated subjects two-compartment PK model first-order absorption (100 mg oral dose, sampled 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 12 h). True parameters: CL = 5 L/h, V1 = 10 L, V2 = 30 L, Q = 10 L/h, ka = 1 h⁻¹; IIV = 0.3 (SD log scale) parameters; proportional error SD = 0.2.

title: Getting started with admixr2
text: Reshape individual records subjects × times matrix, compute E V: V 9×9 sample covariance matrix. -diagonal entries capture within-subject correlation across time; using full matrix (method = "cov") typically tightens parameter estimates compared diagonal-approximation (method = "var"). admixr2 auto-detects method structure V.
code: obs   <- examplomycin[examplomycin$EVID == 0, ] obs   <- obs[order(obs$ID, obs$TIME), ] times <- sort(unique(obs$TIME)) ids   <- unique(obs$ID) n     <- length(ids)                     # 500  dv_mat <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = n, ncol = length(times)) for (i in seq_along(ids)) {   sub         <- obs[obs$ID == ids[i], ]   dv_mat[i, ] <- sub$DV[order(sub$TIME)] }  E <- colMeans(dv_mat) V <- cov.wt(dv_mat, method = "ML")$cov  round(E, 2) #> [1] 0.97 1.94 2.79 3.02 2.26 1.65 1.06 0.75 0.51

title: Getting started with admixr2
text: Models use standard nlmixr2 syntax mu-referenced log-scale parameters: Writing parameter exp(tcl + eta.cl) called mu-referencing: structural fixed effect random effect enter additively log scale. admixr2 exploits pairing compute analytical gradients via sensitivity equations. See Advanced usage vignette details, including parameters without random effect handled.
code: pk_model <- function() {   ini({     tcl     <- log(5)  ; label("Log clearance (L/hr)")     tv1     <- log(10) ; label("Log central volume (L)")     tv2     <- log(30) ; label("Log peripheral volume (L)")     tq      <- log(10) ; label("Log inter-compartmental CL (L/hr)")     tka     <- log(1)  ; label("Log absorption rate constant (1/hr)")     prop.sd <- c(0, 0.2); label("Proportional residual error SD")     eta.cl ~ 0.09     eta.v1 ~ 0.09     eta.v2 ~ 0.09     eta.q  ~ 0.09     eta.ka ~ 0.09   })   model({     cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl)     v1 <- exp(tv1 + eta.v1)     v2 <- exp(tv2 + eta.v2)     q  <- exp(tq  + eta.q)     ka <- exp(tka + eta.ka)     d/dt(depot)      <- -ka * depot     d/dt(central)    <- ka * depot - (cl/v1 + q/v1) * central + (q/v2) * peripheral     d/dt(peripheral) <- (q/v1) * central - (q/v2) * peripheral     cp <- central / v1     cp ~ prop(prop.sd)   }) }

title: Getting started with admixr2
text: Bundle study’s statistics named list:
code: study <- list(   E     = E,   V     = V,                       # full 9x9 covariance matrix   n     = n,   times = times,   ev    = rxode2::et(amt = 100)    # single 100 mg oral dose )

title: Getting started with admixr2
text: Pass one named studies admControl():
code: fit <- nlmixr2(   pk_model, admData(), est = "admc",   control = admControl(     studies   = list(examplomycin = study),     n_sim     = 5000L,     cov_n_sim = 10000L,     maxeval   = 300L,     seed      = 1L   ) )

title: Advanced usage
text: study’s V determines branch NLL used. admixr2 auto-detects method: Override setting method explicitly study list: Use "cov" full covariance matrix expect temporal correlation inform parameter estimates. Use "var" marginal variances available (e.g. published table means SDs) runtime priority.
code: # Force full covariance NLL even for a diagonal V study_cov <- c(study, list(method = "cov"))  # Diagonal approximation when you only have marginal variances study_var <- list(   E      = E,   V      = diag(diag(V)),   # drop off-diagonal entries   n      = n,   times  = times,   ev     = rxode2::et(amt = 100),   method = "var" )

title: Advanced usage
text: admControl() adfoControl() offer gradient strategies via grad argument. adghControl() offers "analytical", "fd", "cfd", "none". adirmcControl() offers "analytical", "none", "fd" ("sens" "cfd"). admControl() gradient modes: adfoControl() gradient modes: adghControl() gradient modes: GH objective noise-free (deterministic quadrature, MC draws), analytical gradient exact resulting Hessian well-conditioned — "analytical" recommended default adgh. adfo, gradient modes still use sensitivity model inside NLL evaluation compute J single rxSolve — grad controls optimizer gradient formed. "sens" recommended standard ODE linCmt() models. sensitivity model unavailable (e.g. rare ODE features), admixr2 falls back "fd" automatically warning.
code: # Analytical gradient (default; fastest for ODE models) fit_sens <- nlmixr2(pk_model, admData(), est = "admc",   control = admControl(studies = list(s = study), grad = "sens",                        n_sim = 5000L, seed = 1L))  # Derivative-free — ignores maxeval; use nloptr stopping criteria instead fit_bobyqa <- nlmixr2(pk_model, admData(), est = "admc",   control = admControl(studies = list(s = study), grad = "none",                        n_sim = 5000L, seed = 1L))

title: Advanced usage
text: Mu-referencing nlmixr2 convention expressing structural parameter fixed effect plus random effect known back-transformation: admixr2 uses pairing classify parameters three cases, handled differently grad = "sens": Paired (mu-referenced) parameters — rxode2 augments ODE system sensitivity equations d(pred)/d(eta_i) random effect. tcl eta.cl enter additively log scale, gradient NLL respect tcl equals gradient respect eta.cl. recovered single ODE solve, extra function evaluations. Non-mu-referenced parameters — structural parameter random effect written separately (e.g. cl <- exp(tcl) * exp(eta.cl)), sensitivity equations keyed etas carry d(pred)/d(tcl). admixr2 therefore emits first-order sensitivity model explicit direction set — one direction per random effect plus one per unpaired structural theta — compiled eventSens = "jump" dosing-modifier (f/lag/rate/dur) sensitivities included. unpaired thetas get analytical gradient single solve, finite differences (message notes sensitivity model carries ). mirrors direction-set scheme nlmixr2est’s fast-focei uses, first order, cross-validated inner model ~1e-13. targeted CRN finite-difference solve used fallback, augmented model built. Parameters without random effect — structural parameter corresponding eta (e.g. v2 <- exp(tv2) IIV dropped V2) simply unpaired case: gets direction sensitivity model exact analytical gradient. (adfo one exception — V_pred = J Ω Jᵀ + Σ needs second derivative dJ/dθ, keeps finite differences structural thetas, noted .)
code: cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl)   # mu-referenced: tcl paired with eta.cl

title: Advanced usage
text: IRMC estimator shifts weights moving proposal mean structural parameter changes. works directly mu-referenced parameters (e.g. cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl)) weight shift closed-form function back-transformed ratio. non-mu-referenced parameters — structural thetas without paired random effect (e.g. ka <- exp(tka) eta.ka) — changing tka inner optimisation alters population mean prediction f(theta, 0) without corresponding weight path. kappa correction accounts adding kappa = f(theta_cand, 0) - f(theta_outer, 0) predicted mean, keeping inner NLL anchored true prediction candidate point. Kappa zero struct thetas mu-referenced. Two methods control f(theta_cand, 0) evaluated inner optimisation: "exact" (default): re-evaluates f(theta_cand, 0) via single rxSolve inner NLL evaluation. Exact; costs one extra rxSolve per inner step. "linearized": precomputes J = df/d(theta) per outer iteration via small FD batch. Approximates kappa_fn(theta_cand) ≈ f0 + J %*% (theta_cand - theta0) — pure arithmetic per inner step, zero extra rxSolve calls. "exact" accurate adds rxSolve every inner NLL call. "linearized" faster approximation good inner steps stay small relative outer box constraint — typical converged phases. Prefer "linearized" complex ODE models rxSolve expensive.
code: adirmcControl(..., kappa_method = "exact")       # default adirmcControl(..., kappa_method = "linearized")  # faster for complex ODE models

title: Advanced usage
text: Multi-restart fitting guards local optima. workers > 1 runs restarts parallel; admixr2 manages worker pool internally: cores distributed automatically: floor(total_cores / n_workers) threads per worker, remainder allocated cyclically (e.g. 13 cores / 4 workers → 4, 3, 3, 3). Workers stopped restart phase cores freed covariance Hessian step. Call admStopWorkers() manually fit interrupted cleanup.
code: fit_par <- nlmixr2(   pk_model, admData(), est = "admc",   control = admControl(     studies    = list(examplomycin = study),     n_sim      = 5000L,     n_restarts = 4L,     workers    = 4L,      # background worker processes (mirai daemons)     cores      = 8L,      # total rxSolve OpenMP threads across workers     restart_sd = 0.3,     # SD of log-scale perturbation from the starting point     seed       = 1L   ) )

title: Advanced usage
text: sampling argument controls eta samples drawn: Sobol sequences typically require 2–5× fewer samples plain normal draws equivalent NLL variance. small n_sim, "lhs" provides better coverage Sobol.

title: Advanced usage
text: covMethod = "r" (default) computes numerical Hessian optimisation reports standard errors print(fit). Important: SEs computed structural residual-error parameters ; omega (IIV) SEs computed (matching nlmixr2 FOCEI behavior). larger cov_n_sim reduces MC noise Hessian: Set covMethod = "none" skip uncertainty estimation early model development need point estimates: Hessian non-positive-definite (SEs printed NA), increase cov_h_outer cov_n_sim.
code: fit_cov <- nlmixr2(   pk_model, admData(), est = "admc",   control = admControl(     studies     = list(examplomycin = study),     n_sim       = 5000L,     cov_n_sim   = 10000L,   # more samples → lower Hessian noise     cov_h_outer = 2.5e-3,   # outer FD step scale (default: eps^(1/5) ≈ 2.5e-3)     covMethod   = "r",     seed        = 1L   ) ) admControl(..., covMethod = "none")

title: Advanced usage
text: Standard information criteria work directly admFit objects. AIC BIC comparable across estimators evaluate likelihood. admc, adgh, adirmc target exact aggregate MVN likelihood (MC, quadrature, importance-weighted estimates integral), information criteria mutually comparable. adfo evaluates linearised likelihood differs , must compared three. compare full model (IIV five parameters) reduced model IIV CL V1 : Lower AIC/BIC favours parsimonious model; difference > 10 generally considered strong evidence.

V_obs must object, sample size n sits outside . Hand likelihood standard error squared told n twice. V = SD^2, never SEM^2.

title: From a published figure to E, V and n
text: 50 mg arm, 60 subjects, digitised concentration–time figure whose caption reads “mean ± SEM” — arm used PD PK/PD data, fitted: bars figure plots sqrt(n) — nearly eight times — smaller standard deviations fit needs. Plotting makes concrete: SEM band far tight -subject spread.  Passing V plain vector variances enough; admixr2 expands diagonal matrix sets method = "var":
code: times <- c(0.5, 1, 2, 4, 8, 12, 24) E     <- c(0.969, 0.920, 0.831, 0.679, 0.460, 0.317, 0.111)     # mg/L SEM   <- c(0.0267, 0.0243, 0.0205, 0.0161, 0.0137, 0.0125, 0.0080) n     <- 60L  SD <- sd_from_sem(SEM, n) round(SD, 3) #> [1] 0.207 0.188 0.159 0.125 0.106 0.097 0.062 band <- rbind(   data.frame(t = times, m = E, lo = E - SD,  hi = E + SD,  what = "mean ± SD (what V needs)"),   data.frame(t = times, m = E, lo = E - SEM, hi = E + SEM, what = "mean ± SEM (what the figure plots)"))  ggplot(band, aes(t, m)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi, fill = what), alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.9, colour = "grey20") +   geom_point(size = 1.8, colour = "grey20") +   facet_wrap(~ what) +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("mean ± SD (what V needs)"          = "#0072B2",                                "mean ± SEM (what the figure plots)" = "#D55E00"),                     guide = "none") +   labs(x = "Time (h)", y = "Concentration (mg/L)") +   theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +   theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),         strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10)) study <- list(E = E, V = SD^2, n = n, times = times,               ev = rxode2::et(amt = 50, cmt = "central")) pk_model <- function() {   ini({     tcl <- log(4)  ; label("Log clearance (L/h)")     tv  <- log(40) ; label("Log volume (L)")     prop.cp <- 0.1 ; label("Proportional residual error")     eta.cl ~ 0.09     eta.v  ~ 0.04   })   model({     cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl)     v  <- exp(tv  + eta.v)     d/dt(central) <- -(cl/v) * central     cp <- central / v     cp ~ prop(prop.cp)   }) } fit <- nlmixr2(pk_model, admData(), est = "adgh",                control = adghControl(studies = list(mg50 = study))) fit ── nlmixr² adgh ──            OBJF       AIC       BIC Log-likelihood adgh -1323.146 -1313.146 -1292.945       661.5729  ── Time (sec fit$time): ──          optimize covariance elapsed other elapsed    0.548      0.056   0.604  3.53  ── Population Parameters (fit$parFixed or fit$parFixedDf): ──                            Parameter    Est.      SE   %RSE tcl             Log clearance (L/h)   1.556 0.01954  1.256 tv                   Log volume (L)   3.911 0.01546 0.3953 prop.cp Proportional residual error 0.09764                        Back-transformed(95%CI) BSV(CV%) Shrink(SD)% tcl         4.738 (4.56, 4.923)     25.9             tv          49.96 (48.47, 51.5)     19.7             prop.cp                 0.09764                          Covariance Type (fit$covMethod): r   No correlations in between subject variability (BSV) matrix   Full BSV covariance (fit$omega) or correlation (fit$omegaR; diagonals=SDs)    Distribution stats (mean/skewness/kurtosis/p-value) available in fit$shrink    Censoring (fit$censInformation): No censoring   Minimization message (fit$message):       NLOPT_XTOL_REACHED: Optimization stopped because xtol_rel or xtol_abs (above) was reached.

title: Generating aggregate data with datagen
text: digitising data publications, study comes model. 2016 paper may used one-compartment model; 2022 follow-may used two compartments. datagen() accepts model element inside study spec overrides top-level default: studies share CL (5 L/h) differ describe distribution: earlier study lumped peripheral distribution single larger apparent volume (V = 40 L), later study resolved two compartments (V₁ = 10 L, V₂ = 30 L, Q = 10 L/h). two studies share drug (CL) different E profiles different dosing distributional assumptions: Simulated aggregate profiles two publications different structural models.
code: # One-compartment oral model (as published in a simpler earlier study) model_1cmt <- function() {   ini({     tcl     <- log(5)     tv      <- log(40)    # apparent volume, peripheral compartment lumped in     tka     <- log(1)     prop.sd <- c(0, 0.25)     eta.cl  ~ 0.04     eta.v   ~ 0.01     eta.ka  ~ 0.01   })   model({     cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl)     v  <- exp(tv  + eta.v)     ka <- exp(tka + eta.ka)     d/dt(depot)   <- -ka * depot     d/dt(central) <- ka * depot - (cl/v) * central     cp <- central / v     cp ~ prop(prop.sd)   }) }  # Two-compartment oral model (as published in a more detailed later study) model_2cmt <- function() {   ini({     tcl     <- log(5)     tv1     <- log(10)     tv2     <- log(30)     tq      <- log(10)     tka     <- log(1)     prop.sd <- c(0, 0.2)     eta.cl  ~ 0.09     eta.v1  ~ 0.04   })   model({     cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl)     v1 <- exp(tv1 + eta.v1)     v2 <- exp(tv2)     q  <- exp(tq)     ka <- exp(tka)     d/dt(depot)      <- -ka * depot     d/dt(central)    <- ka * depot - (cl/v1 + q/v1) * central + (q/v2) * peripheral     d/dt(peripheral) <- (q/v1) * central - (q/v2) * peripheral     cp <- central / v1     cp ~ prop(prop.sd)   }) } lit_data <- datagen(   studies = list(     early_study = list(       model = model_1cmt,       times = c(0.5, 1, 2, 4, 8, 12),       ev    = rxode2::et(amt = 100),       n     = 120L     ),     later_study = list(       model = model_2cmt,       times = c(2, 4, 8, 12, 24),       ev    = rxode2::et(amt = 200),       n     = 180L     )   ),   control = datagenControl(n_sim = 10000L, seed = 2L) ) df_lit <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(lit_data), function(nm) {   s  <- lit_data[[nm]]   sd <- sqrt(diag(s$V))   data.frame(study = nm, time = s$times,              mean = s$E, lo = s$E - sd, hi = s$E + sd) }))  ggplot(df_lit, aes(x = time, y = mean, colour = study, fill = study)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.15, colour = NA) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   geom_point(size = 2.5) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c(early_study = "#009E73", later_study = "#CC79A7")) +   scale_fill_manual(  values = c(early_study = "#009E73", later_study = "#CC79A7")) +   labs(title  = "Literature-derived aggregate profiles",        x = "Time (h)", y = "Concentration (mg/L)",        colour = NULL, fill = NULL) +   theme_bw()

what: Gauss-Hermite moments for design evaluation with adgh
title: Generating aggregate data with datagen
text: method = "fo" matches est = "adfo" inherits FO’s bias nonlinear models large IIV. GH method provides deterministic, noise-free alternative unbiased IIV magnitude matches moments computed est = "adgh": E=∑q=1Qwqf(θ,ηq),V=∑q=1Qwq(fq−E)(fq−E)⊤+Σ E = \sum_{q=1}^Q w_q\,f(\theta, \eta_q), \qquad V = \sum_{q=1}^Q w_q\,(f_q - E)(f_q - E)^\top + \Sigma (ηq,wq)(\eta_q, w_q) Q=mnηQ = m^{n_\eta} tensor-product Gauss-Hermite nodes weights (Cholesky-scaled current Ω\Omega). Like method = "fo" result exact reproducible — stochastic sampling. Use method = "gh" together est = "adgh" design evaluation optimal-design work FO bias non-negligible: MC, FO GH means agree closely IIV moderate model nearly linear η\eta. GH FO diverge MC IIV grows — GH tracks MC accurately linearise ff. number nodes n_nodes (per eta dimension) trades accuracy computational cost: n_nodes = 3 fast; n_nodes = 5 (default) achieves near-exact moments IIV SD ~0.5; n_nodes = 7 extends coverage SD ~0.7.

title: Diagnostic plots
text: "mean" panel 2×2 grid per study. Requires patchwork composite layout; without , four separate plots returned. Mean diagnostics examplomycin study. Top-left — Observed: sample mean ±1 SD ribbon, SD = √diag(V_obs). Top-right — Predicted: predicted mean ±1 SD ribbon, SD = √diag(V_pred). V_pred combines -subject variability (Omega) residual error (sigma). top panels share y-axis scale differences magnitude immediately visible. Bottom-left — Raw residual: E_obs[t] − μ_pred[t] lollipop. grey band ±2 SE(mean), SE = √(V_pred[t,t] / n). Points outside band indicate systematic bias time point. Bottom-right — Standardised residual: z[t] = residual[t] / SE[t]. well-specified model, z approximately N(0, 1). Stars flag |z| > 1.96 (∗), 2.58 (∗∗), 3.29 (∗∗∗). 9 time points multiplicity correction, one flagged time point expected chance alone.

what: Monte Carlo (MC): sample average over η\eta
title: Estimator comparison: adfo, admc, adgh and adirmc
text: admc estimates population integrals directly drawing NN samples ηi∼𝒩(0,Ω)\eta_i \sim \mathcal{N}(0, \Omega) computing sample moments: μpred=1N∑=1Nf(θ,ηi),Vpred=1N∑=1N(f(θ,ηi)−μpred)(f(θ,ηi)−μpred)⊤+Σ \mu_\text{pred} = \frac{1}{N} \sum_{=1}^N f(\theta, \eta_i), \qquad V_\text{pred} = \frac{1}{N} \sum_{=1}^N   \bigl(f(\theta,\eta_i) - \mu_\text{pred}\bigr)   \bigl(f(\theta,\eta_i) - \mu_\text{pred}\bigr)^\top + \Sigma approximation made ff — estimator asymptotically exact N→∞N \\infty. Sobol quasi-random sequences (default) reduce MC variance relative plain normal draws, typically requiring 2–5× fewer samples equivalent precision. key cost every NLL evaluation requires NN rxSolve calls — one per sample. analytical gradient (sensitivity equations mu-referenced parameters , via added first-order direction set, non-mu-referenced structural thetas) adds solves, base cost per optimizer step remains N×N \times (cost one rxSolve). Advantages: Asymptotically exact; AIC directly comparable across admc fits. Works well standard 1–2 compartment PK models moderate IIV. Limitations: NLL evaluation requires NN rxSolve calls; slower adfo. MC noise gradient can cause optimiser oscillation low n_sim.

what: Iterative Reweighting MC (IRMC): proposals fixed, inner loop free
title: Estimator comparison: adfo, admc, adgh and adirmc
text: adirmc addresses main bottleneck admc — need NN new rxSolve calls every optimizer step — decoupling proposal generation optimization. outer phase, NN proposals ηi\eta_i drawn held fixed entire inner optimization. Given fixed proposals, evaluating NLL requires matrix operations — new rxSolve calls. proposals reweighted likelihood current Ω\Omega inner objective remains valid approximation parameters move: wi∝p(ηi∣Ω)q(ηi),μpred=∑iwif(θ,ηi),Vpred=∑iwi(f(θ,ηi)−μpred)(⋯)⊤+Σ w_i \propto \frac{p(\eta_i \mid \Omega)}{q(\eta_i)}, \qquad \mu_\text{pred} = \sum_i w_i\, f(\theta, \eta_i), \qquad V_\text{pred} = \sum_i w_i\,   \bigl(f(\theta,\eta_i) - \mu_\text{pred}\bigr)   \bigl(\cdots\bigr)^\top + \Sigma inner optimizer therefore runs convergence negligible cost; proposals refreshed phases. Box constraints parameters progressively tightened across phases guide global convergence. decoupling makes adirmc particularly well-suited complex ODE models rxSolve expensive: total number solves scales number phases rather number optimizer steps. also robust poor starting values inner optimisation deterministic depend re-sampling. Advantages: Far fewer rxSolve calls per optimizer step admc — especially beneficial complex ODE systems expensive solves. Robust poor starting values; deterministic inner loop. AIC directly comparable admc. Limitations: Heavier per-phase overhead admc simple, well-initialised problems cheap ODE solves.

title: Estimator comparison: adfo, admc, adgh and adirmc
text: adgh evaluates population integrals exactly (accuracy quadrature rule) using tensor-product Gauss-Hermite grid η∼𝒩(0,Ω)\eta \sim \mathcal{N}(0, \Omega). nodes ηq\eta_q weights wqw_q computed via Golub-Welsch algorithm; LηqL\eta_q applies Cholesky factor map standard-normal nodes correlated η\eta space: μpred=∑q=1Qwqf(θ,ηq),Vpred=∑q=1Qwq(f(θ,ηq)−μpred)(⋯)⊤+Σ \mu_\text{pred} = \sum_{q=1}^Q w_q\, f(\theta, \eta_q), \qquad V_\text{pred} = \sum_{q=1}^Q w_q\,   \bigl(f(\theta,\eta_q) - \mu_\text{pred}\bigr)   \bigl(\cdots\bigr)^\top + \Sigma mm nodes per dimension nηn_\eta random effects grid Q=mnηQ = m^{n_\eta} points. objective fully deterministic — MC noise — gradient clean Hessian well-conditioned. Advantages: Noise-free objective — cleaner gradient MC; reproducible across runs without fixing n_sim. AIC directly comparable admc adirmc (likelihood scale). models ≤4\leq 4 etas, QQ small (e.g. 54=6255^4 = 625) adgh substantially faster admc equivalent accuracy. Unbiased IIV magnitude (unlike FO); approximation ff. Limitations: Node count grows exponentially: 55=31255^5 = 3125, 56=156255^6 = 15625. high-dimensional IIV consider admc adirmc. inner-loop cost saving complex ODE systems (unlike adirmc); NLL evaluation runs QQ rxSolve calls.

title: Estimator comparison: adfo, admc, adgh and adirmc
text: model uses mu-referenced parameterisation (cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl)), enables analytical gradient computation via sensitivity equations four estimators. See Advanced usage vignette details.
code: library(admixr2) library(rxode2) library(nlmixr2) library(ggplot2)  data("examplomycin") obs   <- examplomycin[examplomycin$EVID == 0, ] obs   <- obs[order(obs$ID, obs$TIME), ] times <- sort(unique(obs$TIME)) ids   <- unique(obs$ID) n     <- length(ids)  dv_mat <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = n, ncol = length(times)) for (i in seq_along(ids)) {   sub         <- obs[obs$ID == ids[i], ]   dv_mat[i, ] <- sub$DV[order(sub$TIME)] } E <- colMeans(dv_mat) V <- cov.wt(dv_mat, method = "ML")$cov  pk_model <- function() {   ini({     tcl     <- log(5)  ; label("Log clearance (L/hr)")     tv1     <- log(10) ; label("Log central volume (L)")     tv2     <- log(30) ; label("Log peripheral volume (L)")     tq      <- log(10) ; label("Log inter-compartmental CL (L/hr)")     tka     <- log(1)  ; label("Log absorption rate constant (1/hr)")     prop.sd <- c(0, 0.2); label("Proportional residual error SD")     eta.cl ~ 0.09     eta.v1 ~ 0.09     eta.v2 ~ 0.09     eta.q  ~ 0.09     eta.ka ~ 0.09   })   model({     cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl)     v1 <- exp(tv1 + eta.v1)     v2 <- exp(tv2 + eta.v2)     q  <- exp(tq  + eta.q)     ka <- exp(tka + eta.ka)     d/dt(depot)      <- -ka * depot     d/dt(central)    <- ka * depot - (cl/v1 + q/v1) * central + (q/v2) * peripheral     d/dt(peripheral) <- (q/v1) * central - (q/v2) * peripheral     cp <- central / v1     cp ~ prop(prop.sd)   }) }  study <- list(E = E, V = V, n = n, times = times, ev = rxode2::et(amt = 100))

title: The mathematics of aggregate data modelling
text: Meta-analysis pharmacometrics means combining evidence across studies obtain parameter estimates single study support . classical approach pools individual patient data (IPD) multiple studies one dataset. scientifically ideal practically rare: data sharing agreements, proprietary restrictions, simple fact published work reports summary statistics make IPD pooling exception rather rule. Model-based meta-analysis (MBMA) addresses fitting pharmacometric models directly aggregate summaries. summaries come two distinct sources. first directly published: many clinical studies report mean concentration-time profile together variance time point, sometimes full covariance matrix. second model-derived: published pharmacometric model parameter estimates (fixed effects, random-effects variance, residual error) can used generate expected mean vector covariance matrix via simulation, without access original data. covariance structure carries information shape individual profiles variance-summary discard — two studies can identical variances time point yet different correlation structures, reflecting different sources variability. types aggregate data, whether published directly derived published model, can analysed within framework. makes full literature — competitor compounds, historical datasets, regulatory submissions — available modelling input dose selection, trial design, disease-progression modelling. aggregate data likelihood, first systematically described Välitalo (2021), provides statistical foundation MBMA: given nonlinear mixed-effects model, probability observing reported mean covariance? first R implementation admr, described van de Beek et al. (2025). admixr2 re-implements approach within nlmixr2/rxode2 ecosystem, extending new algorithmic ideas focus post.

code: plasma_times <- c(0.5, 1, 2, 4, 8, 12) plasma_mean  <- c(8.793, 7.812, 6.850, 5.597, 3.985, 2.910) plasma_sd    <- c(1.151, 0.911, 0.765, 0.649, 0.770, 0.828)  plasma_study <- list(   E = plasma_mean, V = plasma_sd^2, n = 60L,   times = plasma_times, ev = ev ) pk_plasma <- function() {   ini({     tcl <- log(1);  tv1 <- log(10);  tq <- log(3);  tv2 <- log(8)     prop.cp <- 0.05     eta.cl ~ 0.09     eta.v1 ~ 0.04   })   model({     cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl); v1 <- exp(tv1 + eta.v1)     q  <- exp(tq);           v2 <- exp(tv2)     d/dt(central) <- -(cl/v1)*central - (q/v1)*central + (q/v2)*periph     d/dt(periph)  <-  (q/v1)*central - (q/v2)*periph     cp <- central / v1     cp ~ prop(prop.cp)   }) }  fit_plasma <- nlmixr2(pk_plasma, admData(), est = "adgh",                       control = adghControl(studies = list(trial = plasma_study))) fit_plasma ── nlmixr² adgh ──           OBJF      AIC      BIC Log-likelihood adgh 229.6289 243.6289 270.8316      -114.8144  ── Time (sec fit_plasma$time): ──          optimize covariance elapsed other elapsed    1.226      0.081   1.307  4.46  ── Population Parameters (fit_plasma$parFixed or fit_plasma$parFixedDf): ──              Est.      SE  %RSE Back-transformed(95%CI) BSV(CV%) Shrink(SD)% tcl      0.03322 0.03098 93.27   1.034 (0.9729, 1.098)     27.9             tv1        2.292 0.04514  1.97      9.89 (9.053, 10.8)     15.1             tq         0.883  0.3714 42.06    2.418 (1.168, 5.007)                      tv2       0.8677  0.1541 17.76    2.382 (1.761, 3.221)                      prop.cp 0.004104                              0.004104                          Covariance Type (fit_plasma$covMethod): r   No correlations in between subject variability (BSV) matrix   Full BSV covariance (fit_plasma$omega)      or correlation (fit_plasma$omegaR; diagonals=SDs)   Distribution stats (mean/skewness/kurtosis/p-value) available in $shrink    Censoring (fit_plasma$censInformation): No censoring   Minimization message (fit_plasma$message):       NLOPT_XTOL_REACHED: Optimization stopped because xtol_rel or xtol_abs (above) was reached.

code: # 1. Pull the fitted estimates from the fit (standard nlmixr2 accessors). theta <- fit_cns$theta   # fixed effects (tcl, tv1, tqin, tqout, residuals) omega <- fit_cns$omega   # between-subject covariance (rows/cols already named)  # 2. Simulate the fitted population. We solve a plain, residual-free copy of the #    model so the band shows between-subject variability alone -- and because a #    multi-endpoint fit can only be re-solved with per-endpoint DVID/CMT tags, #    whereas this gives cp and cb directly. sim_model <- rxode2::rxode2({   cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl); v1 <- exp(tv1 + eta.v1)   qin <- exp(tqin); qout <- exp(tqout); vb <- 5   d/dt(central) <- -(cl/v1)*central - (qin/v1)*central + (qout/vb)*brain   d/dt(brain)   <-  (qin/v1)*central - (qout/vb)*brain   cp <- central / v1   cb <- brain / vb }) grid <- seq(0.25, 13, by = 0.25) sim  <- rxode2::rxSolve(sim_model,   params = theta[c("tcl", "tv1", "tqin", "tqout")],   omega  = omega, nSub = 1000L,   events = ev |> rxode2::et(grid), returnType = "data.frame")  # 3. Summarise model and data as mean +/- SD per time, per compartment. band <- function(value)   data.frame(time = sort(unique(sim$time)),              mean = tapply(value, sim$time, mean),              sd   = tapply(value, sim$time, sd))  pred <- rbind(cbind(compartment = "Plasma", band(sim$cp)),               cbind(compartment = "Brain",  band(sim$cb))) obs  <- rbind(   data.frame(compartment = "Plasma", time = plasma_times, mean = plasma_mean, sd = plasma_sd),   data.frame(compartment = "Brain",  time = brain_times,  mean = brain_mean,  sd = brain_sd))  # 4. Model (line + band) over data (points + error bars), coloured by compartment. pal <- c(Plasma = "#0072B2", Brain = "#D55E00")   # Okabe-Ito, colour-blind safe  ggplot() +   geom_ribbon(data = pred,               aes(time, ymin = mean - sd, ymax = mean + sd, fill = compartment),               alpha = 0.15) +   geom_line(data = pred,             aes(time, mean, colour = compartment), linewidth = 1) +   geom_errorbar(data = obs,                 aes(time, ymin = mean - sd, ymax = mean + sd, colour = compartment),                 width = 0.4, linewidth = 0.6, show.legend = FALSE) +   geom_point(data = obs,              aes(time, mean, fill = compartment),              shape = 21, colour = "white", size = 3, stroke = 0.7) +   scale_colour_manual(values = pal, breaks = c("Plasma", "Brain")) +   scale_fill_manual(values = pal, guide = "none") +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 12, 2),                      expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.03))) +   scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.05))) +   coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, NA), clip = "off") +   guides(colour = guide_legend(override.aes = list(linewidth = 1.4))) +   labs(x = "Time (h)", y = "Concentration (mg/L)",        title = "Fitted population prediction vs. observed data",        subtitle = "Line + band: model (mean ± SD)   ·   Points + bars: data (mean ± SD)",        colour = NULL) +   theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +   theme(     legend.position = "top",     legend.justification = "left",     legend.margin = margin(b = 2),     plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),     plot.subtitle = element_text(colour = "grey40", size = 9, margin = margin(b = 9)),     axis.title = element_text(colour = "grey25"),     axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 6)),     axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(r = 6)),     panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),     panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),     panel.grid.major.y = element_line(colour = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),     plot.margin = margin(12, 16, 10, 12)   ) Kp_uu <- exp(theta[["tqin"]]) / exp(theta[["tqout"]]) round(Kp_uu, 2) #> [1] 0.5

title: Multiple studies
text: partition 500 examplomycin subjects two cohorts 250 compute separate aggregate statistics :
code: library(admixr2) library(rxode2) library(nlmixr2) library(ggplot2)  data("examplomycin") obs   <- examplomycin[examplomycin$EVID == 0, ] obs   <- obs[order(obs$ID, obs$TIME), ] times <- sort(unique(obs$TIME)) ids   <- unique(obs$ID)  dv_mat <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = length(ids), ncol = length(times)) for (i in seq_along(ids)) {   sub         <- obs[obs$ID == ids[i], ]   dv_mat[i, ] <- sub$DV[order(sub$TIME)] }  # Alternate subjects into two equal cohorts idx1 <- seq(1, length(ids), by = 2)   # rows 1, 3, 5, ... → cohort 1 idx2 <- seq(2, length(ids), by = 2)   # rows 2, 4, 6, ... → cohort 2  E1 <- colMeans(dv_mat[idx1, ]); V1 <- cov.wt(dv_mat[idx1, ], method = "ML")$cov; n1 <- length(idx1) E2 <- colMeans(dv_mat[idx2, ]); V2 <- cov.wt(dv_mat[idx2, ], method = "ML")$cov; n2 <- length(idx2)

title: Multiple studies
code: pk_model <- function() {   ini({     tcl     <- log(5)  ; label("Log clearance (L/hr)")     tv1     <- log(10) ; label("Log central volume (L)")     tv2     <- log(30) ; label("Log peripheral volume (L)")     tq      <- log(10) ; label("Log inter-compartmental CL (L/hr)")     tka     <- log(1)  ; label("Log absorption rate constant (1/hr)")     prop.sd <- c(0, 0.2); label("Proportional residual error SD")     eta.cl ~ 0.09     eta.v1 ~ 0.09     eta.v2 ~ 0.09     eta.q  ~ 0.09     eta.ka ~ 0.09   })   model({     cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl)     v1 <- exp(tv1 + eta.v1)     v2 <- exp(tv2 + eta.v2)     q  <- exp(tq  + eta.q)     ka <- exp(tka + eta.ka)     d/dt(depot)      <- -ka * depot     d/dt(central)    <- ka * depot - (cl/v1 + q/v1) * central + (q/v2) * peripheral     d/dt(peripheral) <- (q/v1) * central - (q/v2) * peripheral     cp <- central / v1     cp ~ prop(prop.sd)   }) }

title: PD and PK/PD data
text: one-compartment model direct-effect Emax term acting lower DBP. Two observed outputs make multiple-endpoint model, carries residual error term , exactly Several observed compartments: points note: observed output carries residual error term (prop.cp, add.dbp). ordinary nlmixr2 multiple-endpoint syntax. e0 baseline. pre-dose DBP observation , e0 identified extrapolating Emax curve zero concentration. 50 mg arm’s 24 h point, drug contributes 5% emax, keeps extrapolation short. pre-dose observation placebo arm robust way pin baseline; without one, e0 emax trade . drug enters minus sign lowers DBP, emax maximum reduction, mmHg. emax ec50 carry eta: reported DBP SDs nearly flat across time dose, carries little information PD-parameter IIV, eta.e0 already reproduces observed spread.
code: pkpd_model <- function() {   ini({     tcl     <- log(4)  ; label("Log clearance (L/h)")     tv      <- log(40) ; label("Log volume (L)")     te0     <- log(90) ; label("Log baseline DBP (mmHg)")     temax   <- log(10) ; label("Log maximum DBP reduction (mmHg)")     tec50   <- log(1)  ; label("Log EC50 (mg/L)")     prop.cp <- 0.1     ; label("Proportional residual error, concentration")     add.dbp <- 3       ; label("Additive residual error, DBP (mmHg)")     eta.cl ~ 0.09     eta.v  ~ 0.04     eta.e0 ~ 0.007   })   model({     cl   <- exp(tcl + eta.cl)     v    <- exp(tv  + eta.v)     e0   <- exp(te0 + eta.e0)   # baseline DBP     emax <- exp(temax)     ec50 <- exp(tec50)     d/dt(central) <- -(cl/v) * central     cp  <- central / v                          # output 1: concentration     dbp <- e0 - emax * cp / (ec50 + cp)         # output 2: DBP (drug lowers it)     cp  ~ prop(prop.cp)     dbp ~ add(add.dbp)   }) }

Check predicting. ec50 estimated 71% RSE — identified, precisely, dose prediction inherits . temax/tec50 correlation near ±1 mean two trading curve’s plateau identified :
code: theta <- fit$theta cl <- exp(theta[["tcl"]]); v    <- exp(theta[["tv"]]) e0 <- exp(theta[["te0"]]); emax <- exp(theta[["temax"]]) ec50 <- exp(theta[["tec50"]])  conc <- function(dose, t) (dose / v) * exp(-(cl / v) * t) drop <- function(dose, t) { cc <- conc(dose, t); emax * cc / (ec50 + cc) }  # DBP reduction at 1 h, the first sampled PD time round(c(mg50 = drop(50, 1), mg150 = drop(150, 1), mg400 = drop(400, 1)), 1) #>  mg50 mg150 mg400  #>   5.3   9.8  13.3 tt  <- seq(0, 24, length.out = 200) pred <- rbind(   data.frame(t = tt, dbp = e0 - drop( 50, tt), arm = "50 mg (studied)"),   data.frame(t = tt, dbp = e0 - drop(150, tt), arm = "150 mg (predicted)"),   data.frame(t = tt, dbp = e0 - drop(400, tt), arm = "400 mg (studied)")) obs <- rbind(   data.frame(t = pd_times, dbp = dbp50_mean,  sd = dbp50_sd,  arm = "50 mg (studied)"),   data.frame(t = pd_times, dbp = dbp400_mean, sd = dbp400_sd, arm = "400 mg (studied)"))  pal <- c("50 mg (studied)"    = "#0072B2",          "150 mg (predicted)" = "#D55E00",          "400 mg (studied)"   = "#009E73")  ggplot(pred, aes(t, dbp, colour = arm)) +   geom_hline(yintercept = e0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey55") +   geom_line(aes(linetype = arm), linewidth = 1) +   geom_point(data = obs, aes(fill = arm), shape = 21, colour = "white",              size = 2.6, stroke = 0.7, show.legend = FALSE) +   scale_colour_manual(values = pal) +   scale_fill_manual(values = pal) +   scale_linetype_manual(values = c("50 mg (studied)"    = "solid",                                    "150 mg (predicted)" = "22",                                    "400 mg (studied)"   = "solid")) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 24, 4),                      expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.02))) +   labs(x = "Time (h)", y = "DBP (mmHg)", colour = NULL, linetype = NULL,        title = "150 mg predicted from the 50 mg and 400 mg arms",        subtitle = "Points: observed means. Dashed line: baseline.") +   theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +   theme(legend.position = "top", legend.justification = "left",         plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),         plot.subtitle = element_text(colour = "grey40", size = 9,                                      margin = margin(b = 9)),         panel.grid.minor = element_blank()) cv <- fit$cov if (is.null(cv)) {   message("Covariance not computed; refit with a larger cov_h_outer.") } else {   round(cv["temax", "tec50"] / sqrt(cv["temax", "temax"] * cv["tec50", "tec50"]), 3) } #> [1] 0.579

title: Control settings for the FO (First-Order) estimator — adfoControl
text: Creates control object nlmixr2(est = "adfo").  FO estimator linearises model predictions \(\eta = 0\): faster MC estimator less accurate models large IIV strongly non-linear individual predictions.

title: Control settings for the FO (First-Order) estimator — adfoControl
code: adfoControl(   studies = list(),   grad = c("none", "analytical", "fd", "cfd"),   algorithm = NULL,   maxeval = 500L,   ftol_rel = .Machine$double.eps^(1/2),   print = 10L,   seed = 12345L,   cores = rxode2::rxCores(),   nDisplayProgress = .Machine$integer.max,   grad_h = 1e-04,   grad_bounds = 5,   cov_h = 0.001,   cov_h_outer = .Machine$double.eps^(1/5),   covMethod = c("r", "none"),   n_restarts = 1L,   restart_sd = 0.5,   workers = 1L,   rxControl = NULL,   calcTables = FALSE,   compress = TRUE,   ci = 0.95,   sigdig = 4,   sigdigTable = NULL,   addProp = c("combined2", "combined1"),   optExpression = TRUE,   sumProd = FALSE,   literalFix = TRUE,   returnAdmr = FALSE,   ... )

title: Control settings for the FO (First-Order) estimator — adfoControl
text: studies Named list study specifications (format admControl(): E, V, n, times, ev, optional method; observations list multi-compartment fits – see admControl()). grad Gradient mode.  "none" (default) uses derivative-free BOBYQA; "analytical" uses closed-form FO gradient (requires sensitivity equations); "fd" uses forward finite differences full NLL; "cfd" uses central finite differences struct theta gradient (accurate "fd", roughly twice many NLL evaluations per step). algorithm nloptr algorithm, NULL (default) pick default matches grad: "NLOPT_LD_LBFGS" gradient, "NLOPT_LN_BOBYQA" grad = "none". algorithm reported nloptr::nloptr.print.options() accepted. explicit algorithm reconciled grad: grad = "none" gradient-based algorithm (NLOPT_LD_* / NLOPT_GD_*) falls back "NLOPT_LN_BOBYQA"; gradient requested derivative-free algorithm (NLOPT_LN_* / NLOPT_GN_*) turns gradient . emit message. maxeval Maximum function evaluations (default 500). ftol_rel Relative tolerance (default sqrt(.Machine$double.eps)). print Print-frequency live progress (0 = silent). seed Random seed (used restarts). cores OpenMP threads rxSolve(). Defaults rxode2::rxCores(). workers > 1 total budget, split across workers. nDisplayProgress Passed rxSolve(): show solver's text progress bar single solve exceeds many subjects. default (.Machine$integer.max) keeps clean script/vignette output; lower (e.g. 1000L) see progress long fits. grad_h Finite-difference step unpaired struct theta gradient FD Jacobian. grad_bounds Box-constraint half-width using gradients. cov_h Inner FD step gradient-based Hessian (used covMethod = "r" grad != "none"). Default 1e-3. cov_h_outer Outer step scale NLL-FD Hessian. covMethod "r" computes covariance via numerical Hessian structural residual-error parameters (omega/IIV SEs computed, consistent nlmixr2 FOCEI); "none" skips . n_restarts Number optimizer restarts (1 = multi-start). restart_sd Standard deviation random perturbations initial struct thetas restart (> 1). workers Number parallel workers (mirai daemons) multi-restart (default 1 = sequential). Requires mirai package. rxControl rxode2::rxControl() object. Created automatically NULL. calcTables, compress, ci, sigdig, sigdigTable, optExpression, sumProd, literalFix Passed nlmixr2est::foceiControl() table/output machinery. addProp combined additive+proportional error parameterised nlmixr2 output tables: "combined2" (default, variance form) "combined1" (SD form). effect admixr2's estimation. returnAdmr TRUE, return plain list instead full nlmixr2 fit object. ... Unused arguments (trigger error).

what: Control settings for the Gauss-Hermite (GH) quadrature estimator — adghControl
title: Control settings for the Gauss-Hermite (GH) quadrature estimator — adghControl
text: Creates control object nlmixr2(est = "adgh"). GH estimator integrates model predictions random-effects prior \(\eta \sim N(0, \Omega)\) using deterministic tensor-product Gauss-Hermite quadrature grid. unbiased IIV magnitude (unlike FO), noise-free (unlike MC), much faster MC models ~4 etas.

title: Control settings for the Gauss-Hermite (GH) quadrature estimator — adghControl
code: adghControl(   studies = list(),   n_nodes = 5L,   grad = c("analytical", "fd", "cfd", "none"),   algorithm = "NLOPT_LN_BOBYQA",   maxeval = 500L,   ftol_rel = .Machine$double.eps^(1/2),   print = 10L,   seed = 12345L,   cores = rxode2::rxCores(),   nDisplayProgress = .Machine$integer.max,   grad_h = 1e-04,   grad_bounds = 5,   cov_h = 0.001,   cov_h_outer = .Machine$double.eps^(1/4),   covMethod = c("r", "none"),   n_restarts = 1L,   restart_sd = 0.5,   workers = 1L,   rxControl = NULL,   calcTables = FALSE,   compress = TRUE,   ci = 0.95,   sigdig = 4,   sigdigTable = NULL,   addProp = c("combined2", "combined1"),   optExpression = TRUE,   sumProd = FALSE,   literalFix = TRUE,   returnAdmr = FALSE,   ... )

title: Control settings for the Gauss-Hermite (GH) quadrature estimator — adghControl
text: studies Named list study specifications (format admControl(): E, V, n, times, ev, optional method; observations list multi-compartment fits – see admControl()). n_nodes Number quadrature nodes per eta dimension (default 5). Total nodes = n_nodes^n_eta. n_nodes = 5 achieves near-exact covariance moments IIV SD ~0.5; n_nodes = 7 extends coverage SD ~0.7. models >= 5 etas node count grows steeply; consider reducing n_nodes using different estimator. grad Gradient mode. "analytical" (default) uses closed-form contractions sensitivity equations – cheapest exact. "fd" uses forward finite differences; "cfd" uses central FD. "none" uses derivative-free BOBYQA. algorithm nloptr algorithm. title: Control settings for the IRMC estimator — adirmcControl
text: studies Named list study specifications. element list : E – observed mean vector V – observed covariance matrix variance vector (auto-detected) n – sample size times – numeric vector observation times ev – rxode2::et() dosing event table method – "cov" "var" (optional; auto-detected V) Multi-compartment (multiple observed outputs). fit several observed compartments simultaneously (e.g. plasma brain/CSF), give study observations list instead top-level E/V/times. entry one observed output output (model prediction variable, e.g. "cp" "cCSF"), times, E, V – independent fits – ev n. Pass endpoint names admData(), e.g. admData(c("cp", "cCSF")), nlmixr2 recognises every endpoint. two modes: Independent – observed output n/ev (separate experiments / subjects, e.g. plasma study brain study combined meta-analysis). outputs independent likelihood blocks aggregate -2LL sum. Joint (subjects) – outputs measured subjects. Give study shared n ev, joint covariance either study-level full matrix V (blocks observations order) per-output marginal V plus cross list cross-covariance blocks keyed "outA:outB" (length(times_A) x length(times_B); omitted pairs zero). compartments scored single MVN stacked vector shared random effects. est = "adirmc" support multiple observed outputs; use "admc", "adfo" "adgh". Long format (one row per endpoint/time). alternative observations list, study may carry data frame keys observed summary endpoint, way nlmixr2 keys observations DVID/CMT. frame needs endpoint column (DVID, CMT output), time column (TIME), mean column (E) – unless joint V given – variance column (V) SD column (SD). normalised exactly units observations form, two interchangeable:   study-level V (explicit joint = TRUE) marks endpoints -subject; without one, endpoint independent likelihood block. Endpoints stacked order first appear data. n_sim Number Monte Carlo samples per NLL evaluation. outer_iter Maximum inner optimiser iterations per phase. sampling Sampling method eta draws: "sobol" (Sobol, default), "halton" (Halton), "torus" (Kronecker/torus), "lhs" (Latin hypercube), "rnorm" (iid normal). algorithm nloptr algorithm string, NULL (default) pick default matches grad: "NLOPT_LD_LBFGS" gradient, "NLOPT_LN_BOBYQA" grad = "none". algorithm reported nloptr::nloptr.print.options() accepted (e.g. "NLOPT_LD_MMA", "NLOPT_LN_NELDERMEAD"). explicit algorithm reconciled grad: grad = "none" gradient-based algorithm (NLOPT_LD_* / NLOPT_GD_*) falls back "NLOPT_LN_BOBYQA"; gradient requested derivative-free algorithm (NLOPT_LN_* / NLOPT_GN_*) turns gradient . emit message. maxeval Maximum number optimizer function evaluations. ftol_rel Relative function-value tolerance convergence. print Print progress every many evaluations (0 = silent). omega_expansion Inflate proposal Omega factor (>= 1). seed Random seed reproducibility. cores Number OpenMP threads rxSolve(). Defaults rxode2::rxCores(). rxSolve() parallelises subjects, main speed lever MC estimators; workers > 1 total budget, split across workers. nDisplayProgress Passed rxSolve(): solver shows text progress bar single solve exceeds many subjects. default (.Machine$integer.max) keeps bar , want scripts, vignettes logs; lower (e.g. 1000L) see solver progress long interactive fits. grad Gradient mode inner optimiser: "analytical" (default, closed-form weight-path gradient), "none" (derivative-free BOBYQA), "fd" (finite differences). Note: "sens" "cfd" available IRMC estimator. kappa_method Kappa correction method models non-mu-referenced struct thetas: "exact" (default, re-evaluates population prediction f(theta, 0) via rxSolve inner step), "linearized" (precomputes J = df/d(theta) per outer iteration using f(theta, 0) baseline — zero rxSolve per inner step), "linearized_gh" (linear approximation baseline Jacobian use Gauss-Hermite quadrature E_GH[f(theta, eta)] instead f(theta, 0) — accurate baseline IIV magnitude, still zero rxSolve per inner step). kappa_n_nodes Number GH nodes per eta dimension kappa_method = "linearized_gh" (default 5). Total quadrature points = kappa_n_nodes^n_eta. Ignored kappa methods. grad_h Step size finite-difference gradient evaluation optimization (used grad = "fd" "cfd"). default 1e-4 near optimal balance truncation error (grows h) MC noise amplification (grows 1/h) forward FD. Central FD ("cfd") slightly wider optimum around 1e-3, 1e-4 works well . cov_h Inner FD step gradient-based Hessian (used covMethod = "r" grad != "none"). gradient evaluation MC noise order sigma / cov_h; Hessian divides noise outer step, giving total noise sigma / (cov_h * cov_h_outer * |p|). cov_h = 1e-3 balances truncation error noise amplification. Increase 1e-2 Hessian non-positive definite. cov_h_outer Outer step scale numerical Hessian. actual step parameter p max(|p|, 0.1) * cov_h_outer. Applied gradient-FD Hessian (grad != "none") NLL-FD Hessian (grad = "none"). Default eps^(1/5) (~2.5e-3) larger textbook eps^(1/4) account MC noise NLL gradient evaluations; empirically matches analytical (sensitivity-equation) Hessian ground truth. Increase (e.g. 5e-3 1e-2) Hessian non-positive definite. phases Numeric vector box-constraint half-widths, one per phase. Phases progressively tighten search region. convcrit Convergence criterion: phase ends |approx - exact| < convcrit. max_worse Stop phase many consecutive worsening iterations. covMethod Covariance method: "r" (numerical Hessian structural residual-error parameters ; omega/IIV SEs computed, consistent nlmixr2 FOCEI) "none". cov_n_sim Number MC samples covariance (Hessian) step. samples reduce MC noise NLL evaluations. NLL-based Hessian (grad = "none") uses central second difference NLL Sobol sequence (CRN) every perturbed point, noise largely cancels cov_n_sim = 10000 (default) sufficient models. n_restarts Number optimization restarts. Runs parallel workers > 1. restart_sd Standard deviation structural theta perturbations restart initialisation. workers Number parallel workers multi-restart. 1 (default) runs restarts sequentially. Values > 1 run restarts pool background R processes (mirai daemons), behaves way every platform. Requires mirai package. Workers stopped automatically restart phase cores available Hessian step; fit interrupted, admStopWorkers() cleans survivors. rxControl rxode2::rxControl() object. Created automatically NULL. calcTables, compress, ci, sigdig, sigdigTable, optExpression, sumProd, literalFix Passed nlmixr2est::foceiControl() table/output machinery. addProp combined additive+proportional error parameterised nlmixr2 output tables: "combined2" (default, variance form) "combined1" (SD form). effect admixr2's estimation; passed nlmixr2est::foceiControl() table/output machinery . returnAdmr TRUE, return plain list instead full nlmixr2 fit object (useful debugging). ... Additional arguments (none allowed; triggers error).

title: Control settings for the ADM estimator — admControl
text: studies Named list study specifications. element list : E – observed mean vector V – observed covariance matrix variance vector (auto-detected) n – sample size times – numeric vector observation times ev – rxode2::et() dosing event table method – "cov" "var" (optional; auto-detected V) Multi-compartment (multiple observed outputs). fit several observed compartments simultaneously (e.g. plasma brain/CSF), give study observations list instead top-level E/V/times. entry one observed output output (model prediction variable, e.g. "cp" "cCSF"), times, E, V – independent fits – ev n. Pass endpoint names admData(), e.g. admData(c("cp", "cCSF")), nlmixr2 recognises every endpoint. two modes: Independent – observed output n/ev (separate experiments / subjects, e.g. plasma study brain study combined meta-analysis). outputs independent likelihood blocks aggregate -2LL sum. Joint (subjects) – outputs measured subjects. Give study shared n ev, joint covariance either study-level full matrix V (blocks observations order) per-output marginal V plus cross list cross-covariance blocks keyed "outA:outB" (length(times_A) x length(times_B); omitted pairs zero). compartments scored single MVN stacked vector shared random effects. est = "adirmc" support multiple observed outputs; use "admc", "adfo" "adgh". Long format (one row per endpoint/time). alternative observations list, study may carry data frame keys observed summary endpoint, way nlmixr2 keys observations DVID/CMT. frame needs endpoint column (DVID, CMT output), time column (TIME), mean column (E) – unless joint V given – variance column (V) SD column (SD). normalised exactly units observations form, two interchangeable:   study-level V (explicit joint = TRUE) marks endpoints -subject; without one, endpoint independent likelihood block. Endpoints stacked order first appear data. n_sim Number Monte Carlo samples per NLL evaluation. sampling Sampling method eta draws: "sobol" (Sobol, default), "halton" (Halton), "torus" (Kronecker/torus), "lhs" (Latin hypercube), "rnorm" (iid normal). algorithm nloptr algorithm string, NULL (default) pick default matches grad: "NLOPT_LD_LBFGS" gradient, "NLOPT_LN_BOBYQA" grad = "none". algorithm reported nloptr::nloptr.print.options() accepted (e.g. "NLOPT_LD_MMA", "NLOPT_LN_NELDERMEAD"). explicit algorithm reconciled grad: grad = "none" gradient-based algorithm (NLOPT_LD_* / NLOPT_GD_*) falls back "NLOPT_LN_BOBYQA"; gradient requested derivative-free algorithm (NLOPT_LN_* / NLOPT_GN_*) turns gradient . emit message. maxeval Maximum number optimizer function evaluations. ftol_rel Relative function-value tolerance convergence. print Print progress every many evaluations (0 = silent). seed Random seed reproducibility. cores Number OpenMP threads rxSolve(). Defaults rxode2::rxCores(). rxSolve() parallelises subjects, main speed lever MC estimators; workers > 1 total budget, split across workers. nDisplayProgress Passed rxSolve(): solver shows text progress bar single solve exceeds many subjects. default (.Machine$integer.max) keeps bar , want scripts, vignettes logs; lower (e.g. 1000L) see solver progress long interactive fits. grad Gradient mode: "sens" (sensitivity equations, default), "fd" (forward finite differences), "cfd" (central finite differences), "none" (derivative-free). warning issued "sens" requested sensitivity model unavailable; estimator falls back forward finite differences. grad_h Step size finite-difference gradient evaluation optimization (used grad = "fd" "cfd"). default 1e-4 near optimal balance truncation error (grows h) MC noise amplification (grows 1/h) forward FD. Central FD ("cfd") slightly wider optimum around 1e-3, 1e-4 works well . cov_h Inner FD step gradient-based Hessian (used covMethod = "r" grad != "none"). gradient evaluation MC noise order sigma / cov_h; Hessian divides noise outer step, giving total noise sigma / (cov_h * cov_h_outer * |p|). cov_h = 1e-3 balances truncation error noise amplification. Increase 1e-2 Hessian non-positive definite. cov_h_outer Outer step scale numerical Hessian. actual step parameter p max(|p|, 0.1) * cov_h_outer. Applied gradient-FD Hessian (grad != "none") NLL-FD Hessian (grad = "none"). Default eps^(1/5) (~2.5e-3) larger textbook eps^(1/4) account MC noise NLL gradient evaluations; empirically matches analytical (sensitivity-equation) Hessian ground truth. Increase (e.g. 5e-3 1e-2) Hessian non-positive definite. grad_bounds Box-constraint half-width using gradients. covMethod Covariance method: "r" (numerical Hessian structural residual-error parameters ; omega/IIV SEs computed, consistent nlmixr2 FOCEI) "none". cov_n_sim Number MC samples covariance (Hessian) step. samples reduce MC noise NLL evaluations. NLL-based Hessian (grad = "none") uses central second difference NLL Sobol sequence (CRN) every perturbed point, noise largely cancels cov_n_sim = 10000 (default) sufficient models. n_restarts Number optimization restarts. Runs parallel workers > 1. restart_sd Standard deviation structural theta perturbations restart initialisation. workers Number parallel workers multi-restart. 1 (default) runs restarts sequentially. Values > 1 run restarts pool background R processes (mirai daemons), behaves way every platform. Requires mirai package. Workers stopped automatically restart phase cores available Hessian step; fit interrupted, admStopWorkers() cleans survivors. rxControl rxode2::rxControl() object. Created automatically NULL. calcTables, compress, ci, sigdig, sigdigTable, optExpression, sumProd, literalFix Passed nlmixr2est::foceiControl() table/output machinery. addProp combined additive+proportional error parameterised nlmixr2 output tables: "combined2" (default, variance form) "combined1" (SD form). effect admixr2's estimation; passed nlmixr2est::foceiControl() table/output machinery . returnAdmr TRUE, return plain list instead full nlmixr2 fit object (useful debugging). ... Additional arguments (none allowed; triggers error).

Can omitted top-level default supplied via model argument. times Numeric vector observation times. ev dosing event table created rxode2::et(). n (Optional) integer sample size; stored metadata used supplying result admControl(). observations (Optional) named list generate data several observed outputs (multi-compartment). entry gives one output's output (model prediction variable, e.g. "cp"), times, optionally ev/n (inherited study otherwise). present, study result carries matching observations list per-output E/V, ready pass straight admControl(studies = ...). model Optional default model function used study supply model element.  least one model study's model must non-NULL. control datagenControl() object.

title: Generate aggregate study data from (possibly different) pharmacometric models — datagen
text: named list one element per study.  element contains: E Population mean vector times. V Population covariance matrix (length(times) x length(times); ML denominator n_sim method = "mc", analytical FO covariance method = "fo", GH weighted covariance method = "gh"). diagonal carries model's residual-error variance; generate residual-free (IIV-) moments, omit error term model. n Sample size (NA_integer_ supplied). times Observation times. ev Dosing event table. samples Raw n_sim x length(times) prediction matrix (control$return_samples = TRUE).

title: Generate aggregate study data from (possibly different) pharmacometric models — datagen
text: control = datagenControl(method = "mc") (default) population moments computed via Monte Carlo engine est = "admc": $$E_t = \bar{f}_s(\hat\theta_s, \eta_i, t)$$ $$V_{ts} = \widehat{\mathrm{Cov}}_\eta[f_{s,t}, f_{s,s'}] + \Sigma_s$$ \(f_s\) \(\hat\theta_s\) model initial estimates ini() block study \(s\), sample covariance uses ML denominator n_sim, \(\Sigma_s\) diagonal entries determined study model's residual error type (additive, proportional, log-normal). method = "fo" moments instead deterministic First-Order expansion used est = "adfo": $$E = f_s(\hat\theta_s, 0)$$ $$V = J \Omega_s J^\top + \Sigma_s, \quad J_{tj} = \partial f_{s,t}/\partial \eta_j |_{\eta = 0}$$ Jacobian \(J\) obtained sensitivity model (finite differences unavailable). natural choice design evaluation optimal design: moments fast reproducible, data-generating data-analytic models coincide, FO Hessian log-likelihood (expected information matrix) evaluated true maximum rather point MLE generated data. Note est = "adfo" always adds \(\Sigma\) predicted covariance, consistent FIM keep residual error generating model; omit residual-free (IIV-) moments genuinely want. method = "gh" moments computed deterministic Gauss-Hermite quadrature random-effects prior \(\eta \sim N(0, \Omega)\): $$E = \sum_q w_q f(\hat\theta, \eta_q), \quad V = \sum_q w_q (f_q - E)(f_q - E)^\top + \Sigma$$ \((\eta_q, w_q)\) Cholesky-scaled tensor-product GH nodes weights. Unlike FO unbiased IIV magnitude; unlike MC result noise-free exactly reproducible. Matching moments est = "adgh" makes method = "gh" natural choice optimal design estimator. Models compiled cached first use (keyed model expression digest), repeated calls multiple studies sharing model incur single compilation.

Ignored method = "fo" "gh". n_nodes Number Gauss-Hermite nodes per eta dimension method = "gh" (default 5). Total nodes = n_nodes^n_eta. Ignored "mc" "fo". sampling Quasi-random sampling method: "sobol" (default), "halton", "torus", "lhs", "rnorm". Ignored method = "fo" "gh". seed Integer seed.  Applied stochastic methods ("rnorm", "lhs"). Ignored method = "fo" "gh". cores Number rxSolve threads. return_samples Include raw n_sim x length(times) prediction matrix $samples study's output. effect method = "fo" "gh" (methods draw samples).

"mean" "cov" panels returned list also contains sub-panel individually single panel () can extracted code without reprinting whole grid. Elements can pulled name – plot(fit, = "mean")$mean_study1_pred plot(fit, = "cov")$cov_study1_std_resid – position, combined 2x2 grid stored first per study (plot(fit, = "mean")[[1]] full grid, [1] length-1 named sub-list). sub-panel keys <type>_<study>_obs, _pred, _resid, _std_resid; combined grid stays <type>_<study>. extra sub-panel keys printed .

title: Diagnostic plots for an admixr2 fit — plot.admFit
text: "mean" – Observed vs predicted mean per study (2x2 grid). Upper row: observed predicted mean lines +/-1 SD ribbon shared y scale (black throughout). Lower row: raw residual lollipop +/-2 SE band standardised residual z-scores +/-1.96 reference lines. "cov" – Observed vs predicted (co)variance heatmaps per study (2x2 grid). Upper row shares common colour scale (blue-white-red). Lower row uses distinct diverging scales: residual (red-white-green) standardised residual (gold-white-purple). Significance stars overlaid standardised residual panel. "nll" – NLL trace per restart optimizer evaluations. Restarts coloured Okabe-Ito palette. "par" – Parameter trace per restart natural scale (struct thetas back-transformed, sigma SD, omega diagonal variance labelled V(eta.x)). Facets ordered model ini() block. Restarts coloured Okabe-Ito palette.

title: Diagnostic plots for an admixr2 fit — plot.admFit
text: Every admixr2 fit also carries observed predicted aggregate data fit$env$aggData, named list one entry per study. entry holds observation times, study n, two moment sets – obs (data) pred (predicted fitted parameters) – list mean vector E (co)variance matrix V: predicted moments computed one MC simulation fitted parameters using fit's n_sim fixed seed, match default plot(fit) mean/cov panels. slot absent fit simulated (simulation model available).

title: Diagnostic plots for an admixr2 fit — plot.admFit
text: admixr2 fits also plug nlmixr2 traceplot() generic. fitting parameter iteration history best restart stored fit standard parHistData slot (natural scale), traceplot(fit) produces familiar per-parameter, free-y facetted trace used elsewhere nlmixr2 ecosystem. burn-marker (admixr2 records optimizer evaluations, SAEM iterations), best restart shown – per-restart overlay NLL trace remain available via plot(fit, = c("par", "nll")). trace stores improving evaluations (steps lowered best NLL), iter axis indexes improvement steps rather raw optimizer iterations.

title: admixr2 0.3.0
text: Analytical gradients non-mu-referenced (“unpaired”) structural thetas. structural theta mu-referencing eta (tka eta.ka, exp(tcl) * exp(eta.cl) writing style rxode2 mu-reference) used cost extra finite-difference rxSolve per gradient call. admixr2 now emits first-order sensitivity model explicit direction set (one direction per random effect plus one per unpaired theta), compiled eventSens = "jump" dosing-modifier (f/lag/rate/dur) sensitivities longer silently zero. mirrors scheme nlmixr2est’s fast-focei uses (.foceiAnalyticDirections) first-order , cross-validated nlmixr2est’s inner model ~1e-13 across ODE, linCmt, dosing modifiers, initial conditions, covariates, /else multi-endpoint models. Consumed admc, adgh (including joint multi-output studies); adfo keeps finite differences (V_pred = J Omega J' + Sigma needs second derivative). Measured 2.5-3.8x faster ~100x accurate previous finite-difference path 2-compartment model. adds symengine (already hard dependency nlmixr2est, always installed alongside admixr2) Imports, used emit linCmt direction derivatives. feature degrades gracefully rxode2 without eventSens = "jump" support (falls back finite-difference path), minimum-version bump required. Residual error models: pow(), addPow() combined1() now supported, analytical gradients (#84). admixr2 previously supported add, prop lnorm. residual error model now read ui$predDf (errType/errTypeF/transform/addProp) rather iniDf$err alone, every estimator evaluates one shared specification: c = 1 recovering prop b = 0 recovering add. Analytical d(var)/d(sigma), d(mu)/d(sigma) d(var)/d(f) supplied , residual parameters keep exact gradient grad = "sens"/"analytical". Existing add/prop/lnorm fits unaffected: aggregate -2LL bit--bit identical, gradients change floating-point reassociation (~1 ulp). Multi-compartment fitting (multiple observed outputs). study may now observe several model outputs (e.g. plasma brain/CSF) via observations list – one entry per observed output output, times, E V. Two modes (#85): Independent – output n/ev (separate experiments, e.g. literature meta-analysis); aggregate -2LL sum per-output likelihood blocks. Fit full analytical / sensitivity gradients. Joint (subjects) – outputs measured subjects, shared n/ev joint covariance given either study-level full V per-output marginal V plus cross list cross-covariance blocks. Scored single MVN stacked vector shared random effects full analytical gradient three estimators (number compartments; assembled joint covariance checked positive-definiteness). Supported est = "admc", "adfo" "adgh"; datagen() generates multi-output aggregate data plot() renders one panel set per compartment. Pass endpoint names admData(), e.g. admData(c("cp", "cCSF")). est = "adirmc" support multiple observed outputs. Parallel restarts now run mirai daemons. workers > 1 starts pool background R processes instead dispatching future/furrr. replaces previous fork (Unix/macOS) vs PSOCK (Windows/RStudio) split single code path behaves identically every platform, pool lives mirai compute profile never disturbs daemons user set code. furrr future longer used; mirai moves Suggests. Workers still stopped automatically restart phase (now also error/interrupt, via .exit()), cores free covariance step; admStopWorkers() remains available. nDisplayProgress control argument every estimator (admControl(), adfoControl(), adghControl(), adirmcControl()), passed rxSolve() calls drive fitting. sets many subjects single solve must exceed solver shows text progress bar. default (.Machine$integer.max) keeps bar , longer leaks scripts, logs rendered vignettes; lower (e.g. 1000L) watch progress long interactive fits. aggregate-data estimators (adfo, adgh, adirmc, admc) now carry type description attributes classifying “Model Based Meta Analysis” methods, appear category-grouped estimation-method list nlmixr2est prints unsupported est= (bare nlmixr2() call) (#107).

title: admixr2 0.3.0
text: pow() models longer fit wrong residual model, silently. pow(b, c) produces two iniDf rows – coefficient (err = "pow") exponent (err = "pow2"). admixr2 recognised neither, warned , treated additive variances: exponent stored 2*log(c) optimized variance contributing exp(2*log(c)) diag(V). pow model therefore ran completion reported plausible estimates model fitting. Residual parameters now carry role, pow exponent estimated (unconstrained, identity) scale. combined1() honoured. predDf$addProp selects SD-additive (combined1) versus variance-additive (combined2) residual error. admixr2 ignored always computed combined2, dropping 2**b*f cross term. (combined2 nlmixr2’s default, models explicitly asked combined1() affected.) unrepresentable residual model now refused rather approximated. Error types admixr2 express Gaussian aggregate MVN (logitNorm, probitNorm, Box-Cox/Yeo-Johnson transforms, t/cauchy, propF/powF) previously emitted one-time warning treated additive, fit proceeded wrong residual model. now stop(). behaviour change: model “worked” may now error. propT/propF, norm/dnorm dlnorm/logn/dlogn longer emit spurious “modelled …” approximation warnings. aliases, approximations: norm add, logn lnorm, untransformed model propT (scales transformed prediction) exactly prop, transformed untransformed predictions quantity. warnings claimed inaccuracy exist. Lognormal residual error now applied plotted predicted mean. plot.admFit()’s aggregate-data helper added lnorm variance predicted covariance never applied exp(s/2) mean scaling predicted E, lnorm fits plotted mean NLL use. solver progress bar longer appears covariance/gradient batches. internal rxSolve() calls already suppressed , covariance batched-gradient solves admc hard-coded low nDisplayProgress (1000), bar printed chunk exceeded 1000 solves. solves now honour new nDisplayProgress control argument (default ). Hard-coded numeric constants model’s model({}) block (e.g. fixed brain volume vb <- 5, common PBPK/CNS models) longer zeroed. admixr2 used hand-fill every model parameter set 0, clobbering constant’s default producing NA/non-finite objective (e.g. qout / vb divide--zero). now supplies parameters varies lets rxSolve() fill rest model’s defaults, constants covariate defaults keep value. adgh now computes gradients non-mu-referenced (unpaired) structural thetas. unpaired-parameter set derived eta-indexed struct_eta_idx, always empty thetas silently received zero gradient; now uses struct-indexed struct_has_eta. Parallel restarts devtools::load_all() warn installed package. dev mode admixr2 namespace locked, worker daemons run installed package rather loaded source; stale parallel objective silently diverges sequential one. .admRunRestarts now emits one-time warning case telling devtools::install(). never fires production (installed package == source).

title: admixr2 0.3.0
text: Model loading per-fit memory now follow nlmixr2est’s conventions. admixr2 previously pinned fit’s foceiModel companion objects package-level environment (Windows GC-finalizer heap-corruption guard) reclaimed rxode2’s global model registry bespoke snapshot/teardown every fit. gone: companion objects longer pinned (guard proved unnecessary – verified running covMethod = "r" fit path repeatedly aggressive GC crash), estimator now frees memory way nlmixr2est , gc(); rxode2::rxUnloadAll(). disk model cache continues use qs2 + digest, exactly like rxode2/nlmixr2est; -memory pin cache removed (-model reloads come qs2 files). Net: ~290 fewer lines, admixr2-specific memory machinery, fit results unchanged. admClearCache() removed; use rxode2::rxClean(). admixr2’s qs2 caches live rxode2::rxTempDir() alongside rxode2’s nlmixr2est’s, rxode2::rxClean() – rxode2’s standard cache wipe (unload models + clear temp dir), nlmixr2est calls reset – already clears admixr2’s cache . package-specific admClearCache() therefore redundant. print() fit longer writes rmarkdown’s namespace. print.admFit temporarily overwrote rmarkdown:::print.paged_df via assignInNamespace() (restoring .exit) steer nlmixr2est away paged-table branch. branch fact unreachable: nlmixr2est decides paged console output probing behaviour – prints paged_df-classed frame capture.output() infers “paged renderer consumed output” zero captured lines – rmarkdown:::print.paged_df returns knit_asis object visibly print.knit_asis method exists, probe always collects output, always returns FALSE, console branch always taken. stub therefore changed nothing except skipping discarded probe render (~20 ms per print(fit)), cost mutating foreign namespace – fragile, unsafe concurrent rendering, CRAN-policy grey area. Printed output unchanged, byte byte. (#58)

title: admixr2 0.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-07-02

title: admixr2 0.2.0
text: New estimator est = "adgh": deterministic Gauss-Hermite quadrature random-effects prior, configured via adghControl(). objective noise-free (Monte Carlo draws), analytical gradient exact, unbiased IIV magnitude. models ~4 random effects fastest exact estimator (#65). datagen() gains FO-approximated population moments (method = "fo", matching est = "adfo") design evaluation optimal-design work (#56). adirmcControl(kappa_method = "linearized_gh"): GH-averaged kappa baseline IRMC inner loop. admClearCache() prunes session-level compiled-model cache (#10). Control objects now accept nloptr algorithm; default chosen gradient mode, grad/algorithm reconciled automatically (#70).

title: admixr2 0.2.0
text: Fix infinite recursion (“evaluation nested deeply” / “node stack overflow”) aborted first fit R session covariance matrix requested (covMethod = "r"). Accessing ui$simulationModel left self-referential compiled-model object ui$meta, nlmixr2’s ui-cloning fit assembly traverse. admixr2 now clears transient artifact .admLoadModel(), keeping ui canonical state nlmixr2 expects. Affected four estimators (adfo/admc/adgh/adirmc) (#81). Use ML denominator (1/n_sim) consistently MC gradient kernels, matching NLL (#48). Fix parallel multi-restart dispatch fork/PSOCK, fix adirmc multi-restart (#45). Guard non-positive predicted variance diagonal-NLL paths (#57). Correct FO diagonal omega gradient scaling, plus assorted plot, output-variable detection, caching, worker-serialization fixes.

title: admixr2 0.2.0
text: Add Gauss-Hermite sections across vignettes fix pkgdown reference index documentation site builds (#79).

title: admixr2 0.2.0
text: Declare minimum versions imported rxode2 (>= 5.1.2) nlmixr2est (>= 6.0.1), suggested nlmixr2 (>= 5.0.0) (used examples tests).

title: admixr2 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-06-02 Initial release. Monte Carlo estimator (est = "admc") via admControl(). Iterative Reweighting Monte Carlo estimator (est = "adirmc") via adirmcControl(). Analytical CRN gradient sensitivity equations (grad = "sens"). Multi-restart parallelism via furrr/future. Diagnostic plots: observed vs predicted mean/covariance, NLL trace, parameter trace. traceplot() support: admixr2 fits populate standard parHistData slot, nlmixr2 traceplot() generic works natively (best restart, natural scale, burn-marker). Integrates nlmixr2/rxode2 ecosystem.
